name: Maebius, Clayton
resolved_employer: President, Maralo, LLC (Austin-based oil & gas exploration and production company, 1717 W. 6th St. Ste. 470, Austin 78703); Texas attorney (Bar #207708, licensed 2001, status inactive)
evidence: Maralo LLC's own site lists Clayton Maebius as President under CEO Mary Ralph Lowe; the firm is an oil & gas company founded on Ralph Lowe's late-1930s oil exploration, drilling its first well in 1940. Maralo's office address is 1717 W. 6th St. Ste. 470, Austin, TX 78703 — matching the donor's zip 78703 exactly — and the filing's employer strings ('Maralo, LLC','Maralo LLC') and occupation ('Investments') align. The State Bar of Texas lists Clayton Yost Maebius with firm 'Maralo LLC', Austin, licensed 11/02/2001, now inactive and reporting 'Non-Law-Related Employment', consistent with running the oil & gas company rather than practicing. Notable: gave to Austin council member Paige Ellis and Kirk Watson; 3 FEC gifts at 0.0 partisan lean (uniformly Republican federally).

affiliations.role: President (oil & gas exploration and production company)

name: Ross, Shirley
resolved_employer: District Manager, Wells Branch Municipal Utility District (north Austin MUD); 25+ years in utility management and environmental consulting, formerly Environmental Affairs Manager and Regional Operations Manager at Southwest Water Company (Houston)
evidence: Wells Branch MUD's own staff directory lists Shirley Ross as District Manager, overseeing daily operations of all departments, supervising personnel, and advising the Board of Directors — matching the filing's employer ('Wells Branch MUD') and occupation ('District Manager') exactly. Holds a BS in Biological Sciences from Michigan Technological University and an MPH in Urban Health from UT. Classified Government because a MUD is a Texas political subdivision. Residential zip 78750 (Anderson Mill) is adjacent to but not the same as the district she manages, and was not independently confirmed; employer/occupation/title align precisely. Gave to Kirk Watson and Travis County Commissioner Brigid Shea, consistent with a local-government utility manager. 248 FEC gifts at 0.236 partisan lean (predominantly Republican federally).

name: Liu, Frank
resolved_employer: President and founder, Lovett Commercial (Houston commercial development/redevelopment firm); also founder of Lovett Homes, InTownHomes and Lovett Industrial
evidence: Frank Liu is the founder and president of Lovett Commercial, which he established in 1995 after founding Lovett Homes in 1980 — matching the filing's employer ('Lovett Commercial, LLC') and occupation ('president') exactly, with Houston zip 77003 consistent with Lovett's East End Houston base and project portfolio. Born in Taiwan, raised partly in Vietnam, emigrated to the U.S. in 1971 and to Houston in 1973; Rice University civil engineering graduate. Best known for converting the 550,000 sq ft former Barbara Jordan Post Office into POST Houston. An out-of-town Houston developer giving to Austin council members Ryan Alter and Chito Vela fits a real-estate-development interest pattern. No FEC donation history on record.

name: Gaston, Brian
resolved_employer: President, The Gaston Company (Austin commercial real estate investment and development firm, 7600 Burnet Rd. Ste. 210, Austin 78757), founded 1986
evidence: Brian Gaston is President of The Gaston Company, a licensed commercial real estate professional with 25+ years of experience specializing in commercial real estate investment and development — matching the filing's employer ('The Gaston Company') and occupations ('President','Investor') exactly. Duke mechanical engineering graduate with a Harvard MBA; involved with the Rotary Club of Austin and the Real Estate Council of Austin, and former President of the Ronald McDonald House. The Austin Chamber directory lists the firm under Real Estate & Construction / Real Estate - Commercial. Donor zip 78766 is an Austin PO-box zip and was not independently tied to him; company city and employer/occupation strings align. 2 FEC gifts at 1.0 partisan lean (Democratic).

name: Yerly, Rebecca
resolved_employer: Project Analyst, TXP, Inc. (Austin economic analysis and public policy consulting firm, 1310 S. 1st St.), with the firm since 1999; previously case design analyst at WaxmanCavnerLawson
evidence: TXP's own team page lists Rebecca Yerly as a Project Analyst with the firm since 1999, working on public-policy projects including microlending in economic development, urbanist development and regional growth, and the earned income tax credit in the San Antonio region — matching the filing's employer ('TXP, Inc.') and occupations ('Researcher','Administrator') exactly. TXP's office at 1310 S. 1st St. sits in zip 78704, matching the donor's zip. Holds a Ph.D. in Educational Psychology from UT. She and her husband Jon Hockenyos (TXP's founder) have advocated at the Texas Legislature to expand insurance mandates and opportunities for adults with autism, earning Texas Senate recognition. 40 FEC gifts at 1.0 partisan lean (uniformly Democratic), consistent with giving to Kirk Watson.

name: Spector, Susan
resolved_employer: Retired; self-reported occupation 'Financial' (former career field not independently identified)
evidence: No web source could be found for a Susan Spector in Austin zip 78749 — searches of news, obituaries, LinkedIn and professional directories returned no corroborating match. The Finance classification rests solely on the donor's own disclosure in the campaign finance filing, which reports occupation 'Financial' and employer 'Retired' with address Austin, TX 78749-4110; per the retiree rule this is recorded as Finance (retired). Treat as self-reported and uncorroborated: the specific individual, her former employer, and her career field have not been independently verified. Single $1,000 gift to Travis County Commissioner Margaret Gomez (Feb 2022); no FEC donation history.

name: Scott, John
evidence: Could not identify. 'John Scott' is an extremely common name and the filing carries no zip, location, occupation or employer. The FEC record attached to this donor is unusual — 1,894 federal gifts at a 0.198 partisan lean, i.e. a very high-volume, predominantly Republican federal donor (the volume suggests small recurring contributions) — yet the local gift is $1,000 to Democratic Travis County Commissioner Ann Howard (Oct 2021). That combination is notable but is not by itself identifying, and no candidate individual could be corroborated. Deliberately not matched to the Republican former Texas Secretary of State of the same name, who was appointed in the same month but has no evident tie to this gift. Not classified.
